tasks.title: Email Late Physics Lab Report to Prof. Vance
tasks.context: Missed the deadline yesterday due to a power outage. Prof agreed to accept for partial credit if sent before midnight.
tasks.whyImportant: I need to pass Physics to keep my university scholarship.
tasks.obstacle: I get overwhelmed writing apology emails and procrastinate until it's too late.
tasks.workspaceDraft:
Dear Professor Vance,

I apologize for the delay in submitting my Physics Lab Report. As mentioned earlier, my area experienced a severe power outage yesterday which prevented me from completing and compiling the data in time.

I have attached the complete report here for your review. I deeply appreciate your willingness to accept it for partial credit.

Sincerely,
Alex Johnson
tasks.motivationNudge: You mentioned this assignment helps you keep your scholarship. You are only 45 seconds away from sending it and ending the anxiety. Let's execute!

tasks.title: Review Ancient Mesopotamia & Nile Civilization for Midterm
tasks.context: Need to memorize key timelines, societal systems, and river irrigation strategies for units 1-4.
tasks.whyImportant: I want to graduate with honors and maintain my 3.8 GPA.
tasks.obstacle: I keep scrolling Instagram because ancient history feels dense and dry.
frameworkPoints: Mesopotamia: Focus on Tigris/Euphrates flooding unpredictability, Hammurabi's Code (legal unification), and Cuneiform.
Nile Civilization: Predictable flooding cycles, highly centralized pharaonic administration, and monumental construction techniques.
Comparative Angle: Contrast decentralized Mesopotamian city-states with unified, secure Egyptian kingdoms.

studyCards.front: What is Hammurabi's Code and why is it significant?
studyCards.back: The earliest known comprehensive legal code, written in cuneiform. It unified Mesopotamian city-states under a standardized system of retributive justice ('eye for an eye').

studyCards.front: How did Nile flooding shape Egyptian administration?
studyCards.back: Predictable flooding allowed for highly planned crop seasons. This led to a large, centralized bureaucracy tracking land surveys, taxation, and large state granaries.

studyCards.front: Contrast Tigris/Euphrates vs Nile water control.
studyCards.back: Mesopotamia: Violent, unpredictable flooding required aggressive canal and dyke maintenance. Egypt: Gentle, predictable seasonal swelling led to natural basin irrigation systems.

tasks.motivationNudge: You mentioned Instagram distracts you. Set a timer for just 15 minutes of flashcards. Instagram will still be there, but your GPA will be safer.

tasks.title: Restock Empty Fridge (Healthy Groceries)

tasks.obstacle: I'm usually too tired after work and order greasy takeout.
